title: Pedilonum moirianum
intro: Pedilonum moirianum, commonly known as the Australian wattle or Pedilanthus, is a plant species native to Australia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Pedilanthus include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Pedilanthus is prepared as a tea or decoction, and is also used in herbal remedies such as infusions and tinctures.
taxonomy: Pedilonum moirianum belongs to the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family.
